codeReview.summary: The Task boundary modification API (obs-modify-scope) was fully implemented by commit 207c83a in the observability-operator-intervention feature. The worker for obs-modify-scope (session 9cec613c) correctly verified existing implementation and confirmed all tests pass. Implementation includes: TaskScope struct with files, directories, and allowed_operations fields; original_scope and current_scope fields on Task; modify_scope() and restore_original_scope() on TaskStateMachine; async modify_scope() and restore_original_scope() on Orchestrator; CliCommand::TaskModifyScope in daemon; and 4 comprehensive tests in swell-daemon.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly identified that the feature was already fully implemented by a previous worker (commit 207c83a). No new implementation was required. The worker followed proper verification procedure by running cargo build, cargo test with filter, and cargo clippy.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows 'The Task boundary modification API (obs-modify-scope) was already fully implemented by a previous worker' - this is accurate since the implementation was added in commit 207c83a (feat(swell-daemon): add operator intervention APIs).

summary: obs-modify-scope feature review passes. The Task boundary modification API was fully implemented in commit 207c83a as part of the observability-operator-intervention feature. Worker session 9cec613c correctly verified existing implementation and ran verification steps (cargo build --release, cargo test -p swell-daemon -- modify_scope, cargo clippy on swell-orchestrator and swell-daemon). All 4 modify_scope tests pass. Implementation includes TaskScope struct, original_scope/current_scope tracking on Task, modify_scope() and restore_original_scope() methods, async Orchestrator APIs, and CliCommand::TaskModifyScope handler. No blocking issues found.
